Dog:
Avoid: chocolate, xylitol, grapes / raisins, onion, garlic, paracetamol (acetaminophen), ibuprofen, naproxen.
Use with care: NSAIDs (carprofen / meloxicam OK at dog doses), benzodiazepines in some breeds.
Note: dose by weight.
Cat:
Avoid (lethal): paracetamol / acetaminophen, ibuprofen, aspirin (chronic), permethrin / pyrethroids, lilies (all parts), essential oils (tea tree, etc.).
Use with care: NSAIDs (meloxicam single dose only).
Note: stress-prone, hide-handle gently. Cats lack glucuronidation.
Rabbit:
Avoid (fatal enterotoxemia): oral penicillin, amoxicillin, ampicillin, lincomycin, clindamycin, cephalexin, erythromycin. Also fipronil. Mineral oil.
Safe abx: enrofloxacin, trimethoprim-sulfa, metronidazole, chloramphenicol.
Note: hindgut fermenters — fragile GI motility. Anorexia >12h is emergency. Fragile spine on handling.
Hamster:
Avoid (fatal): oral penicillin, amoxicillin, ampicillin, lincomycin, erythromycin (enterotoxemia). Streptomycin (paralysis).
Note: very tiny — dose calculation is critical. Wet tail (proliferative ileitis) is common emergency.
Gerbil:
Avoid: penicillin family, lincomycin, streptomycin.
Note: prone to seizures; minimize handling stress.
Chinchilla:
Avoid: oral penicillin, amoxicillin, ampicillin, lincomycin, cephalexin (enterotoxemia).
Safe abx: enrofloxacin, TMS, ceftiofur (SC).
Note: heat-sensitive (>25 °C dangerous). Requires dust bath. GI stasis emergency similar to rabbit.
Guinea Pig:
Avoid (fatal): oral penicillin, amoxicillin, ampicillin, lincomycin, erythromycin, cephalosporins.
Safe abx: enrofloxacin, chloramphenicol, TMS.
Note: requires daily Vitamin C (cannot synthesize). Anorexia is emergency.
Ferret:
Avoid: aspirin, ibuprofen.
Use with care: standard NSAIDs cautiously.
Note: very prone to insulinoma, adrenal disease, lymphoma. Heartworm susceptible — preventative needed. Hypoglycemia risk — never fast long.
Hedgehog:
Avoid: ivermectin in some reports (sensitivity).
Note: attempted hibernation if <18 °C is a medical emergency. Self-anointing is normal. WHS (Wobbly Hedgehog Syndrome) is degenerative.
Sugar Glider: Note: calcium-deficient diet causes hindlimb paralysis (HLP). Self-mutilation if isolated — social species. Need protein + Ca + vit D3.
Squirrel: Note: metabolic bone disease common if calcium-deficient. Many species are legally protected — verify ownership status.
Gatsby: Note: uncommon species — verify medication safety before any treatment. Stress-prone. Confirm appropriate diet, husbandry, temperature.
Snake:
Avoid: ivermectin in indigo snakes / some species (verify).
Note: anesthesia has long induction / recovery. Brumation period in winter affects appetite. Mites + RI (respiratory infection) common.
Tortoise:
Avoid (often lethal in chelonians): ivermectin, fenbendazole at high dose.
Note: Vitamin A deficiency common. UVB lighting essential. Hibernation must be pre-planned + weighed in/out. Shell metabolic disease common.
Turtle:
Avoid: ivermectin (often lethal in chelonians).
Note: water quality is primary therapy. Shell rot from chronic damp. Aural abscess common in box turtles.
Gecko: Note: Metabolic bone disease very common — calcium dust feeders, UVB. Tail autotomy is normal stress response.
Chameleon: Note: Extremely stress-prone — minimize handling. Drink only droplets (mist), not bowls. UVB + humidity critical. Calcium ± D3 essential.
Iguana: Note: Metabolic bone disease very common. STRICT herbivore — no animal protein. UVB essential. Aggressive when sexually mature.
Bearded Dragon: Note: Metabolic bone disease very common — check Ca / P, UVB, diet. Atadenovirus screening. Yellow fungus.
Monitor Lizard: Note: Large meals — careful gastric content with anesthesia. Powerful bite. Salmonella carrier.
Bird:
Avoid (lethal fumes): Teflon / PTFE cookware, aerosols, scented candles. Avocado. Chocolate. Aspirin caution.
Use with care: NSAIDs (meloxicam OK in many).
Note: mask induction quick. Air sac anatomy — never restrict sternum. STRESS KILLS — minimize handling.
Parrot:
Avoid: Teflon / PTFE fumes, avocado, chocolate, alcohol, caffeine.
Note: Heavy metal toxicity common (zinc, lead from cages / toys). Sex hormones drive screaming + plucking. Long-lived (40–80y).
Chicken:
Avoid in laying hens: ivermectin (egg residue), tetracyclines, fluoroquinolones (most have egg withdrawal).
Use with care: ALL antibiotics have egg / meat withdrawal — record withdrawal date on prescription.
Note: Marek's disease, respiratory infections, egg-binding common.
Duck:
Avoid in laying: ivermectin, tetracyclines (egg withdrawal).
Note: Aspergillosis prone (warm damp). Bumblefoot from hard surfaces. Need water deep enough to submerge head.
Pigeon: Note: Trichomoniasis (canker), coccidiosis common. Paramyxovirus (PMV-1) requires screening before flocking. Ectoparasites common.
Fish: Note: Anesthesia via tricaine (MS-222). Water quality + parameters is primary medicine — test before treating.
Amphibian:
Avoid: latex gloves (use nitrile, rinsed). Direct sunlight. Detergent residue on hands.
Note: Permeable skin — meds absorbed dermally. Chytrid (Bd) screening for new arrivals.
